name: Microsoft Prompt Flow
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Azure AI Studio integration adds cloud costs

auth.notes: Local: no auth; Azure AI Studio: Azure credentials

performance.notes: Framework overhead; actual latency dominated by LLM calls

agent_readiness.notes: Microsoft's LLM application development framework. Define flows as DAGs of Python/Jinja2 nodes. Evaluation framework for testing prompt variants. Tracing with OpenTelemetry. Azure AI Studio integration for cloud deployment. VS Code extension. Batch testing across datasets. Connection management for LLM APIs. MLflow integration. Used in enterprise Azure AI workflows. More structured than LangChain but less flexible.

gotchas: YAML-heavy DAG definitions can be verbose
Best features require Azure AI Studio (adds vendor lock-in)
More opinionated than LangChain — harder to customize
VS Code extension required for best development experience
Evaluation framework is limited compared to DeepEval/Ragas
best_when: Azure-centric LLM applications needing structured evaluation
Enterprise teams standardizing LLM development workflow
DAG-based prompt chains with clear input/output types
Teams using Azure AI Studio for deployment
avoid_when: Teams preferring Python-native over YAML-based definitions
summary: Microsoft Prompt Flow is an LLM application development framework using DAG-based flow definitions. Includes evaluation, tracing, and Azure AI Studio integration. Best for enterprise Azure teams standardizing LLM development and evaluation. More structured but less flexible than LangChain. Best features require Azure AI Studio; standalone OSS use is functional but limited.
